dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 5818 IH: Country of Origin Labeling Enforcement Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Hageman (for herself, Mr. Khanna, Mr. Davidson, Mr. Massie, Mr. Roy, and Mr. Gosar) introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Agriculture

form.official-title: To amend the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 to prohibit retailers from designating the United States as the country of origin of foreign beef, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Country of Origin Labeling Enforcement Act of 2025.

section.header: Country of origin labeling for beef

subsection.text: Section 281 of the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 (7 U.S.C. 1638) is amended—

paragraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (1) through (7) as paragraphs (2) through (8), respectively;
by inserting before paragraph (2) (as so redesignated) the following:
The term beef means meat produced from cattle (including veal).
in paragraph (2)(A)(i) (as so redesignated), by striking lamb and venison and inserting beef, lamb, and venison; and
in paragraph (2)(A)(ii) (as so redesignated), by striking ground lamb and ground venison and inserting ground beef, ground lamb, and ground venison.

subsection.text: Section 282(a)(2) of the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 (7 U.S.C. 1638a(a)(2)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in the paragraph heading, by inserting beef, after for;
in each of subparagraphs (A) through (D), by inserting beef, before lamb each place it appears; and

subparagraph.text: in the subparagraph heading, by inserting beef, after Ground; and
by inserting ground beef, before ground lamb each place it appears.

subsection.text: Section 283(b) of the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 (7 U.S.C. 1638b(b)) is amended by striking $1,000 for each violation and inserting $1,000 for each violation (or in the case of a covered commodity that is beef, $5,000 for each pound of beef not in compliance with the requirements of section 282).

No ruling by the World Trade Organization or by any other international organization of which the United States is a member that is established before, on, or after the date of enactment of this Act may be construed to limit, alter, or affect the authority of the Secretary of Agriculture to require country of origin labeling in accordance with the amendments made by this section.
